items.headline: U.S. Stocks Show Mixed Performance Amid Geopolitical Tensions and Economic Data Anticipation
items.summary: U.S. stock futures climbed early Tuesday as reports circulated that President Donald Trump is considering an end to the Iran war, leading to a temporary easing of oil prices and improved investor sentiment. Major indices demonstrated mixed performance in Monday's regular trading, with the S&P 500 and Nasdaq Composite declining, while the Dow Jones Industrial Average posted a modest gain. Investors are now keenly awaiting key economic data, including the March consumer confidence index and February's JOLTS job openings report. The broader market remains influenced by the ongoing Middle East conflict, sticky inflation concerns, and a notable softness in chip stocks. Not financial advice.

items.statContext: Up 0.93% on Tuesday. Closed Monday down 0.39% at 6,343.72.
Up 1.14% on Tuesday. Closed Monday up 0.11%.
Up 1.09% on Tuesday. Closed Monday down 0.73%.

items.headline: Artelo Biosciences ($ARTL) Surges on Private Placement Closure
items.summary: Shares of Artelo Biosciences Inc. ($ARTL) gained over 23% in pre-market trading after the company announced the closure of its $11 million private placement of common stock.

items.headline: Bullfrog AI ($BFRG) Continues Ascent with Pharma Deal
items.summary: Bullfrog AI Holdings Inc. ($BFRG) soared more than 12% in pre-market, extending Monday's 107% gain, following an agreement with a major global pharmaceutical company.

items.headline: Hitek Global ($HKIT) Doubles Pre-Market on Offering Closure
items.summary: Hitek Global Inc. ($HKIT) more than doubled, gaining 110% in pre-market trade, as its $3 million registered direct offering of Class A ordinary shares closed.

items.headline: Virgin Galactic ($SPCE) Rises Despite Mixed Q4 Results
items.summary: Virgin Galactic Holdings ($SPCE) saw a 9% increase in shares, despite reporting mixed Q4 results. The surge was driven by improved adjusted EBITDA due to lower operating expenses.

items.headline: Sysco ($SYY) Slumps Following Restaurant Depot Deal
items.summary: Sysco ($SYY) slumped 15.3% after announcing its Restaurant Depot deal, which investors reportedly viewed more as a financing headache than a beneficial acquisition.

items.headline: UnitedHealth ($UNH) Slides on Revenue Decline Forecast
items.summary: UnitedHealth ($UNH) shares slid as the company anticipates its first annual revenue decline in over 30 years, with 2026 revenue projected down about 2% from 2025.

items.headline: Trump's Stance on Iran War Boosts Futures, Oil Prices Fluctuate
items.summary: U.S. stock futures gained on Tuesday after reports indicated President Donald Trump is willing to end the Iran war even if the Strait of Hormuz remains closed, easing some geopolitical anxieties. However, oil prices continued to fluctuate and remain elevated, with Brent crude around $111.35 per barrel and WTI at $101.78 per barrel as of Monday's close.

items.headline: Fed's Powell Signals Patience Amid Inflation Concerns
items.summary: Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell reiterated that long-term U.S. inflation expectations remain well-anchored and that the current policy stance allows officials to 'wait and see' the economic impact of the Iran conflict. He noted no immediate need to raise interest rates, despite the likelihood of higher inflation in the coming months due to the oil shock.

items.headline: Investors Await March Consumer Confidence and February JOLTS Data
items.summary: Markets are keenly focused on upcoming economic data releases today, including the consumer confidence index for March and the JOLTS (Job Openings and Labor Turnover Survey) report for February. These reports will provide further insights into the U.S. labor market and consumer sentiment amidst current economic uncertainties.

items.headline: Energy and Materials Lead as Sectors Shift, Tech Under Pressure
items.summary: Energy and materials sectors are showing leadership today, a reversal from their 2025 performance. Conversely, technology stocks, particularly chip manufacturers like Nvidia ($NVDA) and Intel ($INTC), remained under pressure. The airline industry and financial services also faced headwinds due to higher fuel costs and global economic uncertainty.

items.headline: Major Companies Reporting Earnings Today
items.summary: Several major companies are scheduled to release their earnings reports today, March 31, 2026, including Nike ($NKE), Beyond Meat ($BYND), Bit Digital ($BTBT), Bitfarms ($BITF), and Dave & Buster's ($PLAY). Investors will be watching these reports for insights into corporate performance and future outlook.
